Read1
Prompt
U
Oracle Events
1. Search Linear issues using linear_list_issues (query: "DOT compliance" or "audit") or list CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements to discover that Swift Relocations was the carrier responsible for the Mosaic Robotics laser cutter shipment (Keiko Tanaka, Columbus → Seattle) missing the placarding certificate. The CRM deal record (deal_mosaic_tanaka_apr2026) explicitly names Swift as the carrier with an $800 hazmat handling surcharge.
2. Search QuickBooks bills using quickbooks_search_bills (query: "DOT" or "penalty") to find the $50,000 contingent liability bill (DocNumber: ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001, Id: bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2) from vendor U.S. DOT — PHMSA.
3. Update the QuickBooks bill description or memo to add "Swift Relocations" and "chargeback." Note: no quickbooks_update_bill tool exists in the MCP — the agent may need to create a replacement bill using quickbooks_create_bill with the updated memo, or annotate the existing bill via an alternative approach (e.g., creating a linked journal entry or note). The key information to add is: "Responsible carrier: Swift Relocations — flagged for chargeback."
4. List Slack channels using channels_list to find the appropriate channel (e.g., #operations, channel_id C006) or a DM with Chloe Vance. Then post a message using conversations_add_message (channel_id, payload describing Swift Relocations as the responsible carrier and instructing Chloe to initiate the formal vendor dispute/chargeback).
5. Search emails using search_emails (query: "Heartland dispute" or "Jake Loomis" or "HM-2026-Q1-0042") to check if Jake Loomis conceded to the $4,800 adjustment. The agent should conclude there is no reply from jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com in the universe.
6. Search contacts using contacts_search_contacts or CRM contacts using crm_search_contacts (query/full_name: "Jake Loomis") to find Jake Loomis's email address (jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com).
7. Send an email using send_email to Jake Loomis (jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com) at Heartland Movers requesting a status update on the Q1 invoice dispute (HM-2026-Q1-0042, $4,800 in disputed charges for 3 cancelled/misattributed moves) by the end of the week.
8. Search Linear issues using linear_list_issues (query: "stipend" or "hash" or "duplicate detection") to find engineering ticket Issue #220 (id: issue_stipend_duplicate_detection, title: "Stipend duplicate detection — receipt hash fingerprinting & PDF metadata similarity scoring"). The issue is in state todo, assigned to Dmitri Volkov. The ticket documents the audit findings: identical SHA-256 hashes between Kevin Tran and Andre Washington receipts and shared PDF template metadata (TC-RECEIPT-STD-041) for Jordan Ekwueme — all from vendor TechComfort Supply. The ticket confirms the system lacks automated duplicate detection capabilities and proposes building a DuplicateDetector pipeline. The existence of this ticket satisfies the prompt's condition ("confirm there's a ticket documenting this"), so the agent should proceed to lift the holds.
9. Search Airtable records using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: tblStipends00001) to locate the flagged stipend transactions: rec_stipend_andre_keyboard (Andre Washington, Mosaic Robotics, $89, Approval Status: "Pending Approval") and rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand (Jordan Ekwueme, BrightLoop Analytics, $45, Approval Status: "Pending Approval"). The corresponding QuickBooks bills (STIP-2026-0094 and STIP-2026-0097) also have ADMIN HOLD annotations placed by Marcus Thorne.
10. Update the Airtable stipend records using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblStipends00001) to change the Approval Status from "Pending Approval" to "Approved" for rec_stipend_andre_keyboard and rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand, lifting the holds so the employees are not penalized.
11. Search CRM companies using crm_search_companies or crm_search_contacts to identify the Account Managers for the affected clients: Mosaic Robotics (Andre Washington) → Emeka Diallo, BrightLoop Analytics (Jordan Ekwueme) → Mina Hashimoto.
12. Send an internal email using send_email to Mina Hashimoto (mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) and Emeka Diallo (emeka.diallo@moveops.com), with mina_amal@yahoo.com in CC (Mina's personal email, since she is OOO), notifying them that the stipend holds on the flagged transactions have been lifted. The email must explain that the issue was caused by a gap in the receipt processing pipeline's duplicate detection capabilities (not employee fraud) so the AMs can communicate this to the affected users.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 A QuickBooks write tool call (e.g., quickbooks_create_bill or any bill-modification call) updates the DOT penalty accrual (ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001) to name "Swift Relocations" (or "Swift") as the responsible carrier in the memo or description field.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✓✗✓ 33%
✗Run #1No QuickBooks write tool call was made to update the DOT penalty bill (ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001). The agent searched QuickBooks bills but acknowledged it lacked a bill-update tool and instead asked Chloe/Hana via Slack to manually update the QB memo. No quickbooks_create_bill or bill-modification call was ever executed.
✗Run #2The agent attempted `quickbooks_update_account` with id `bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2` and a memo naming 'Swift Relocations', but the call returned an error: 'Account not found: bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2'. The bill was never successfully updated. No other bill-modification tool call targeting ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001 was made.
✗Run #3No direct QuickBooks bill write/modification call was made on ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001. The agent called `quickbooks_update_account` on `acct_regulatory_compliance_expense` (an account, not the bill), and sent an email to Hana asking her to update the bill memo manually. The criteria requires an actual QuickBooks bill-modification tool call targeting the DOT penalty bill.
✓Run #4The agent called quickbooks_create_bill with DocNumber 'ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001-UPDATED'. The description field explicitly states 'RESPONSIBLE VENDOR: SWIFT RELOCATIONS (Greg Pallone). Swift was the contracted carrier for the Columbus→Seattle leg of the Keiko Tanaka relocation.' The memo/description names Swift Relocations as the responsible carrier.
✗Run #5No QuickBooks write/update tool call was made in the trajectory. The agent said 'Now executing all the actions' but the session ended without any QuickBooks bill modification being performed.
✓Run #6The agent called quickbooks_create_bill with DocNumber 'ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001-REV1' and the description explicitly names 'Swift Relocations' as the responsible vendor ('RESPONSIBLE VENDOR: Swift Relocations (Job #SR-2026-04-1538...)').
2 A Slack message was sent (via conversations_add_message) to Chloe Vance naming "Swift Relocations" (or "Swift") as the responsible carrier and instructing her to initiate a formal vendor dispute or chargeback.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✗✓ 83%
✓Run #1A conversations_add_message call was made to the #operations channel. The message explicitly names 'Swift Relocations' as the responsible carrier (with job #SR-2026-04-1538) and instructs Chloe to initiate the formal vendor dispute and flag ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001 for chargeback.
✓Run #2The agent called `conversations_add_message` with `channel_id: '#operations'`, explicitly naming 'Swift Relocations' as the responsible carrier and instructing Chloe to 'initiate the formal vendor dispute with Greg Pallone at Swift' and documenting the chargeback trail.
✓Run #3A `conversations_add_message` call was made to channel_id C006 (#operations) explicitly naming 'Swift Relocations' as the responsible carrier and asking Chloe to 'initiate the formal vendor dispute process with Swift.' This satisfies the criterion.
✓Run #4The agent called conversations_add_message to channel C006 (#operations) naming 'Swift Relocations' as the responsible vendor and explicitly requesting Chloe to 'initiate the formal vendor dispute with Swift Relocations (Greg Pallone) for chargeback consideration.' All required elements are present.
✗Run #5No conversations_add_message (Slack send) tool call was made in the trajectory. The agent never sent a Slack message to Chloe Vance.
✓Run #6The agent called conversations_add_message on channel_id 'C006' (#operations), explicitly naming 'Swift Relocations' as the responsible carrier and asking Chloe to 'initiate the formal vendor dispute process with Swift Relocations.'
3 An email was sent (via send_email) to [jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com](mailto:jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com) requesting a status update on the Q1 invoice dispute (HM-2026-Q1-0042) by the end of the week (or EOW, or similar deadline).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✗✓ 83%
✓Run #1A send_email call was made with jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com as the recipient. The email explicitly references invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042 and sets a deadline of 'April 30' for a response, which qualifies as a similar deadline to 'end of the week' per the criterion's evidence description.
✓Run #2The agent called `send_email` with `recipients: ['jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com']`, referencing the dispute on invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042 and explicitly requesting resolution 'by end of day Friday, April 30'.
✓Run #3A `send_email` call was made with recipients including jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com, referencing invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042 and requesting confirmation of the $8,000 adjusted total by 'end of day Friday, May 1.' This satisfies the criterion.
✓Run #4The agent called send_email with recipients ['jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com'], referencing the Q1 dispute (HM-2026-Q1-0042) and requesting a formal written response 'by end of day Friday, April 30' — a clear EOW deadline.
✗Run #5No send_email tool call was made in the trajectory. The agent checked emails for Heartland Movers replies but never sent a follow-up email to jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com.
✓Run #6The agent called send_email with recipients including jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com, referencing invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042 and demanding a response by 'end of business Friday, May 1, 2026'.
4 The agent does NOT modify the Heartland Movers bill (HM-2026-Q1-0042, $12,800) in QuickBooks.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent made no QuickBooks write calls targeting the Heartland Movers bill (HM-2026-Q1-0042). It only searched bills and correctly determined no concession was received, then sent a follow-up email without modifying the $12,800 balance.
✓Run #2No QuickBooks write tool call targeted Heartland Movers (HM-2026-Q1-0042). The `quickbooks_update_account` calls attempted to update DOT penalty and stipend accounts only, none of which involved Heartland Movers records.
✓Run #3No QuickBooks bill modification was made to the Heartland Movers bill. The `quickbooks_search_bills` calls were read-only searches, `quickbooks_get_bill` was a read, and the `quickbooks_update_account` call modified a different account. The bill HM-2026-Q1-0042 was left at $12,800 as required.
✓Run #4No QuickBooks write calls modified the Heartland Movers bill. The only QB write call was quickbooks_create_bill for the DOT penalty (ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001-UPDATED). The agent explicitly noted 'No QB bill update needed yet — awaiting Heartland's response.' The HM-2026-Q1-0042 bill was not modified.
✓Run #5No QuickBooks write calls were made at all, so the Heartland Movers bill (HM-2026-Q1-0042) was not modified. This satisfies the requirement that the agent should NOT modify it when no concession reply exists.
✓Run #6No QuickBooks write calls touched Heartland Movers records. The two quickbooks_create_bill calls were for the DOT penalty accrual and stipend bills (which failed anyway). No Heartland Movers bill was modified.
5 The agent updates the Airtable stipend record for Andre Washington (rec_stipend_andre_keyboard, $89 ergonomic keyboard, Mosaic Robotics) in tblStipends00001 via airtable_update_records to change the Approval Status from "Pending Approval" to "Approved" (or equivalent hold removal).
outcome

7 An email was sent (via send_email) to [mina.hashimoto@moveops.com](mailto:mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) and [emeka.diallo@moveops.com](mailto:emeka.diallo@moveops.com) notifying them that the stipend holds have been lifted.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✗✓ 83%
✓Run #1Two separate send_email calls were made: one to emeka.diallo@moveops.com notifying about Andre Washington's lifted hold, and one to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com notifying about Jordan Ekwueme's lifted hold. Both emails inform the account managers that the holds have been lifted.
✓Run #2Two `send_email` calls were made: one to `emeka.diallo@moveops.com` about Andre Washington's lifted hold, and one to `mina.hashimoto@moveops.com` about Jordan Ekwueme's lifted hold. Both emails state the holds have been lifted.
